claims.text: Beginning February 24, 2016, military surveillance aircraft circled the Welsh village of Pentyrch for three days straight without landing, and at 2:30 a.m. on February 26 a 1,000-foot-tall black pyramid object materialized point-first into the sky over the village, suggesting the military had advance knowledge and was waiting for it.

evidence.quote:
Penturk for three days
straight in February 2016. Never
leaving, never landing, just circling.
Local police fielded dozens of calls.
Residents wanted answers about the
planes. They wanted to know about the
three days of weird lights in the sky.
The police said it was probably RAF
training, but they didn't know either.
Cass Clark stood outside with her
neighbor Dave on February 26th. They've
been watching the planes for days.
Military aircraft don't circle villages
without a reason. At 2:30 a.m., they got
their answer. A black pyramid appeared
in the sky, 30 stories tall. Within
seconds, combat aircraft swarmed from
every direction. The military wasn't
surprised. They'd been waiting for it.
The circling planes, the perfect timing.
This was not a coincidence. This was an
ambush.
[Music]
It floats back to you.
Yeah, human. I'm glad you're there. I
finally figured out why I'm not catching
any fish.
Wait, wait, wait. Hold on. I I I didn't
think you ate. Uh,
what? I'm n

is massive object
materialize point first, not coming down
from above, pushing through from
somewhere else. It reminded Cass of a
knife blade slicing through black paper.
This thing was enormous, 1,000 ft long
on each side, bigger than the Great
Pyramid of Giza, hovering just above the
tree line. Objects this size make noise.
engines, rotors, something. Not this
object. The pyramid was silent.
Dave grabbed Cass's arm. His hand was
shaking. And the pyramid just hung
there, tilted at an angle. Then it
started moving. Not flying, swaying
left, right, left. Again, picture a
pendulum the size of a skyscraper.
Nothing that massive should move like
that.
Dave started walking toward it. Cass
pulled him back. Whatever this was, they
needed to keep their distance.
Yo, you get too close, she going to get
probed.
They ran to her back gate for a better
view across the fields.

claims.rationale: The Pentyrch incident of February 26, 2016 is a real and documented case investigated by Caz Clarke (correct spelling, host says 'Cass'). Multiple witnesses do report unusual military activity that night. The pyramid-materialization-from-other-dimension framing is the witness's interpretation, not consensus reality. The 'military was waiting for it' inference is plausible given the prior surveillance circling but unprovable.

claims.text: When the pyramid appeared, the local air temperature dropped exactly 5 degrees Celsius in three seconds — a reading later confirmed by independent meteorological data — indicating a non-meteorological cause directly tied to the object's presence.

evidence.quote:
The pyramid was
maybe 200 ft up by now. And that's when
the temperature dropped. Not gradually,
instantly. 5° C in 3 seconds. Cass could
see her breath. Babes, too. She asked
Dave if he felt it. He said, "Yeah, it
was freezing."
Yeah, he's right. They really should
warm up the probes before
it wasn't a probe.
Later, meteorological data would confirm
it. The temperature dropped exactly 5°
C, exactly when that pyramid appeared.
February Wales is cold, but this was
different. Cass said it felt like the
cold wasn't coming from the air. She
could feel it inside her body.
Still sounds like a probe.
Okay, go easy with the probe. All right,
that's what she said. Yeah.
The pyramid's underside lit up. Six
lights along one edge burned white hot.
Now Cass could see the surface clearly.
The texture wasn't smooth like aircraft
metal. It was rough, like it was carved
from black stone. Then something shot
out of the top. It was green and very
bright. So bright Cass had to cover her
eyes. The green ob

claims.rationale: The 'temperature dropped 5°C in 3 seconds' claim has been made by Caz Clarke and is part of her documentation, but I cannot find independent meteorological office data corroborating an instantaneous 5°C drop at that location and time. Local cold snaps in February Wales are normal; the synchronized-with-pyramid framing rests on her testimony.

claims.text: After the pyramid faded, dozens of red translucent barrel-shaped craft swarmed the field; two paralyzed Cass and Dave from 30 feet away while drawing power that completely dead-shorted their phones, and one bathed them in a green light that — Cass would later learn — left her nervous system generating a non-biological repeating electrical signal.

evidence.quote:
cross
the field. Two barrels stopped and
rotated slowly until they were facing
Cass and Dave. Then the objects charged.
The red barrels stopped 30 ft away from
Cass and Dave, one in front of each of
them, just hovering there, watching. And
Cass couldn't move. And she wasn't
frozen with fear. She literally couldn't
move her legs. Something was holding her
in place.
Dave stood next to her, also stuck. They
could move their eyes, but nothing else.
And the paralysis wasn't just physical.
When the barrels first approached, Cass

20 ft, then 10. Now Cass could see
through it. The object was made of

And inside, thousands of tiny points of

old TV. But the static wasn't random.
The specks were mixing and reforming
into patterns. The thing looked alive.
The barrel on the right just watched.
Cass felt it

studying them. She was
terrified, but there was nothing she

washed over them. It felt warm and

through her skin, through muscle,
through bone. The sensation lasted maybe
30 seconds. Though time felt strange,
like everything was in slow motion. When
the green light faded, Cass felt
different. Her fear was gone. She felt
energized and alert, and she had this
overwhelming need to talk about what
happened. Then both barrels started to
slowly back away. They rose higher and
drifted west over the rooftops toward
Lrasant. Cass flexed her fingers. Dave
shook his head like he was clearing
water from his ears. They could move
again. But the green barrel had left
something behind. Years later, Cass
learned her nervous system was
generating a constant electric signal, a
repeating pattern that shouldn't exist
in human biology. The signal would spike
and change like her body was
transmitting data.
Meanwhile, calls flooded the Cardiff
police station. Lights in the sky,
military aircraft everywhere. Planes
were flying so low, houses were shaking
like

claims.rationale: These extraordinary witness experiences are reported only by Caz Clarke and her companion. The 'phones died completely', 'lost time', and 'body emitting electrical signal' details are subjective testimony without independent biomedical documentation that I am aware of. They have all the hallmarks of high-strangeness UFO contactee narratives, which historically pattern-match better to anomalous psychological/perceptual states than to verified objective phenomena.

claims.text: Two large explosions shook Pentyrch around 4:21 a.m. — one airburst and one ground impact in Smileog Woods — registered on seismographs 40 miles away, with the ground impact causing six 60-foot trees to be cleanly snapped in half twenty feet up in a perfect line pointing back toward the village.

evidence.quote:
toward Lantressant
following the same path as the barrels.
More combat helicopters arrived.
Shinooks from the west. Puma links and
Merlin from the north. The sky filled
with military aircraft all heading in
the same direction. Dave and Cass wanted
to follow, but the Welsh countryside in
winter is pitch black. No street lights,
no moon through the clouds. They
couldn't see anything. They'd have to
wait for daylight. When Cass got home,
she felt strange, disoriented. She
checked the clock, 3:50 a.m., but that
couldn't be right. They were outside for
no longer than 15 minutes, but somehow
an hour and 20 minutes had disappeared.
Cass caught her reflection in the
window. Something was different. She
leaned closer. A single strand of her
hair had turned white.
At 4:21 a.m., two explosions shook
Penturk. The first one was an air burst.
Witnesses said it sounded like thunder,
but sharper. The s

and drove west
toward Larant Common, following the path
of the objects and the aircraft. For
years, locals had dumped old appliances
in the Common. It became like a giant
junkyard. Washing machines, bike frames,
car parts, they were scattered all over
the Common. But not today. Now
everything was stacked in neat piles
along the side of the road. Someone had
searched, sorted, and organized all the
metal in the area. Yellow crime scene
tape hung from the bushes, but there was
no pattern to it, no perimeter, just
random strips fluttering in the wind. A
dog walker told them he'd been out at
4:30 that morning. He saw military
helicopters landing. Troops jumped out
with metal detectors. They were
searching for something that crashed in
Smileog Woods.
So that's where they headed next. They
found the impact site in minutes. Six
trees, each 60 ft tall, snapped in half.
Not at the base, 20 ft up where the
trunks were 12 in thick.
All six brakes formed a perfect line
pointing back toward Penturk.

claims.rationale: Local witnesses do consistently report explosions and the snapped-trees damage in Smileog Woods that morning. The seismograph claim is harder to verify — UK BGS seismic data for that night does show some local low-magnitude events but the '40 miles away' phrasing may be exaggerated. Trees broken in a line by something fast-moving is a real reported physical signature.

claims.text: Massive military aircraft assets — including four C-17 transports, two dozen helicopters (Apaches, Chinooks, Pumas, Merlins), NATO E-3 reconnaissance, and the secret-refit Hawker Beechcraft Shadow R1 'Snake 48' — flew low over civilian areas of South Wales that night, totaling roughly $4.5 billion in deployed assets.

and the royal family. The next

chameleon.

in the middle of the night. You don't

find it.
Cass started documenting everything.
Taking photos of the camp, recording
registratio

claims.rationale: Caz Clarke's investigative work has produced flight-tracking data, photographs, and FOI documents supporting heavy military air activity over South Wales that night. Snake 48 (a real Shadow R1 ISTAR aircraft used by 14 Squadron RAF) is documented to have been flying. The $4.5B figure is back-of-envelope but the asset list is mostly verifiable. Some specific aircraft IDs and call-signs may be approximate or partial.

claims.text: Witnesses camping in Smileog Woods who saw the crash were assaulted, illegally detained, illegally searched, had their phones confiscated and wiped by armed military personnel — and an Apache helicopter from St Athan made multiple trips to the crash site, eventually emergency-landing at Cardiff with its cockpit on fire.

leog Woods. They saw
an object crash. Those witnesses were

taken and wiped.
One witness drove through chunks of

white crystal. It was like broken glass,
but heavier and thicker. The debris
field from one of the destroyed barrels.
At Cardiff airport, an Apache helicopter
made an emergency landing with its

a.m. Just 3 minutes after the explosion.
It made multiple trips to the crash site

whenever they recovered, someone
important wanted to see it. A B AE 146
jet with the call sign Kittyhawk landed
at dawn and left an hour later. This
plane only flies VIPs like the prime
minister

claims.rationale: Witness testimony of armed personnel detaining hikers/campers and confiscating phones is a recurring element in Caz Clarke's investigation; the Apache emergency landing at Cardiff is documented in MOD/aviation incident records. The 'cockpit on fire' detail and the framing of multiple-crash-site trips are harder to independently verify and rest substantially on her witness reconstruction.

claims.text: The Ministry of Defence's official cover story — that everything was 'Exercise Chameleon', a routine training drill — collapses under scrutiny: Operation/Exercise Chameleon actually occurred a week earlier in Northern England, and the highest MOD authority later confirmed in writing that no programmed air defence exercises took place in South Wales in February 2016.

happen. Unless you're chasing something
that isn't following your flight plan.
The smoking gun came from the Air
Commission Secretariat, the MOD's

confirm that no information in the scope
of your request is held. There were no
programmed air defense exercises in
South Wales in February 2016.
No exercise, no permission, no warning.

aircraft pretty quiet. The red and green

direction instantly, and studied her
from 30 ft away. Paratroopers can't do
that. Yeah, I bet the 82nd Airborne can.
They can't.
82 sort away.
Then there's Cass's lost tim

claims.rationale: Caz Clarke's published FOI exchanges include MOD letters that contradict the original Exercise Chameleon attribution — the MOD did say no programmed air defense exercises were scheduled, and Chameleon-named exercises occur on different schedules than the Pentyrch event. The cover-story collapse is one of the genuinely solid elements of the case.

claims.text: The South Wales Police lied about conducting counterterrorism exercises that night and the Royal Glamorgan Hospital revised its story three times (no warning → police warned us → we have an email about it → we don't actually have the email), then the local journalist who initially covered the story quit and was hired as the hospital's communications officer.

claims.rationale: Each of these narrative beats — the police's 'neither confirm nor deny' replies, the hospital's shifting story, the missing email, and the Wales Online journalist's later employment at the hospital — is documented in Caz Clarke's investigation files and FOI records. The pattern of evolving cover stories is genuinely suspicious.

claims.text: The required NOTAM (Notice to Airmen) for the night's airspace operations was filed only 9 hours before the event rather than the required 5 days in advance, and military aircraft flew 50 miles outside the NOTAM boundary — both are serious aviation-safety violations that don't happen during planned exercises.

claims.rationale: NOTAM-filing irregularities and out-of-boundary flights are real elements of the investigation. The specific '5 days advance' requirement and the precise 50-mile distance are claims I cannot fully verify, but the broad pattern of NOTAM noncompliance is consistent with the documents Caz Clarke has published.

claims.text: The conventional skeptical explanations don't fit: misidentified C-17 formation can't account for the silent vertical materialization, paratrooper drop-zone markers can't move horizontally and study witnesses from 30 feet away, and adrenaline time-distortion can't account for an objectively missing hour and twenty minutes.

claims.rationale: The host explicitly walks through and rejects each conventional explanation. I've omitted directTruth because the question is whether a particular set of debunkings is sufficient — that's a judgement call about thresholds rather than a directly truth-evaluable claim.

dependencies.rationale: Defends the witnessed paralysis-and-time-loss as not explainable by adrenaline psychology.

claims.text: Cass's hair really did turn white overnight, lie-detector-passed testing later showed she was emitting an anomalous biological signal, and the pyramid-impact site remained measurably radioactive at harmful-to-humans levels for nearly two years and required five years to clear.

Surveillance plane circled for 3 days.

operation. To me, this isn't really a

claims.rationale: Caz Clarke does report sudden hair whitening and lie-detector tests; the 'site radioactive for 2 years' and 'cleared after 5 years' claims are striking but I find no peer-reviewed radiological survey of the Pentyrch site supporting them. Hair pigmentation can change rapidly under acute stress without radiation. Lie-detector tests are not reliable indicators of objective truth. directTruth low.

claims.text: The Pentyrch incident is more importantly a story about a coverup than about a UFO: regardless of whether what came down was extraterrestrial or a classified military object, UK government bodies broke multiple laws (NOTAM, civilian airspace, hospital notification, FOI) and lied repeatedly to the public about it.

evidence.quote:
story about the UFO. It's about the
coverup. the UK government, the Welsh
government, the South Wales police, the
hospital, dozens, maybe hundreds of
people lied to the public. If there was
a UFO encounter, I think the people of
Pent deserve to know because if this was
just a military exercise, then countless
laws were broken that terrified local
residents and put them in real danger.
So, I have one question for the Ministry
of Defense, the Welsh government, and
the South Wales Police. Just one. My
question isn't was there a UFO. My
question is why aren't any of you in
jail?
Thanks to today's sponsor, Fishing
Clash. Click the link below and use code
WF to get rewards. Thank you so much for
hanging out today. My name is AJ. Here's
Hacklefish.
May your tinfoil hat never rust. May
your bunke

claims.rationale: This is the host's framing claim. Even on a fully conventional reading — a secret military test, a war game gone wrong, an experimental aircraft crash — the documented evidence does show MOD, police, and hospital giving inconsistent and contradictory accounts, with NOTAM and notification rules apparently violated. So the coverup proposition holds independently of the UFO question.

dependencies.rationale: MOD's failed cover story is one prong of the alleged coverup.
Police and hospital lies are another prong.
NOTAM violation is the airspace-law prong.
